name: Howard McGhee
abstract: Howard McGhee (March 6, 1918 – July 17, 1987) was one of the first American bebop jazz trumpeters, alongside Dizzy Gillespie, Fats Navarro, and Idrees Sulieman. He was known for his fast fingering and high notes.

about_quotes.speaker: Earl Watkins
about_quotes.evidence: And here you have Dizzy Gillespie, Howard McGhee, Carl George, and another fella... Then I can’t remember the rest of the saxophone section. Mingus on the bass. And there I am sitting up there. And so we did several tunes for Black & White.
about_quotes.doc_title: Earl Watkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Howard McGhee... We made his second outing. And that's where I made what I later on found out to be a classic recording, 'Up In Dodo's Room,' with Howard McGhee's band.

about_quotes.speaker: Percy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: I was playing with Howard McGhee in the first international jazz festival in Paris.
about_quotes.doc_title: Percy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Lanny Morgan
about_quotes.evidence: I was on a band that Howard McGhee put together for a couple of years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lanny Morgan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: Howard McGhee is listed in the recording personnel for 'Cherokee' by Charlie Barnet and His Orchestra, but the interviewee does not mention a personal interaction or direct collaboration with McGhee in the provided text.

about_quotes.speaker: Big Nick Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: That's the first time I ever heard Howard McGhee... I says, 'when I came to Cincinatti, you were there with Lucky Gay from Milwaukee... I wasn't but 16, but you were grown.'
